question: Under joint traces composing the two verdicted player models — VERDICT 001's channel-activity tiers (low/med/high + paced-spam farmer) and VERDICT 008's grid playstyles (casual roamer, deep-runner, `!fastmine` grinder) into single-player profiles (chat-only and grid-only regression legs FIRST, each reproducing its verdict's solo headline rates, then mixed-casual, mixed-deep, and a cross-surface arbitrage farmer that fills either surface's cooldown dead time with the other surface's actions), which cooldown-namespace contract — (a) per-source clocks at the pinned defaults (CHAT_ACTIVITY 900 s / GRID_ROAM 600 s, combined per-player ceiling 10/hr), (b) one shared per-player clock (c in {600, 900} s), (c) per-source clocks plus a combined per-player hourly cap (K in {4, 6, 8}) — keeps BOTH verdicts' pinned shapes simultaneously true (wild: rare-but-visible per traffic tier under the 5.22/hr debounce x threshold ceiling; grid: zero above depth threshold, rare-but-present at depth; farming unprofitable in RATE terms on each surface) while bounding the combined per-player interruption rate at or under the stricter solo ceiling (the Q-0087 never-mandatory bound), and does cross-surface cooldown arbitrage buy the farmer a combined encounter-rate yield materially above the honest mixed profiles' — i.e. do the two solo-tuned default sets survive coexistence unchanged, or does a named default or the namespace rule itself need re-pinning before the first build fixes the engine?

source.proposal_pin: idea-engine main d1d1b6b5575c2dce084a41325fd2a3a9a9e5ab66 (landed via idea-engine PR #279)
source.shared_seam: superbot-games games/shared/encounter/interface.py @ 64b3371b5ca240bcbaea3833acba6fdd2d627ef6 (fixture byte-copy; zero cooldown/rate surface — verified)
source.premise_window: superbot live HEAD cdb26804ea4a61c5c1d09ddaea46a4c535175dc5 at 2026-07-13T00:45:05Z (moved from the proposal's 0f991a8 pin by a docs-only delta: 3 files, 360 insertions, no code); no Encounters cog, no spawn engine, no grid-encounter slice; disbot/utils/mining/grid.py rules 'v1 = free movement, NO encounters' — the window is still OPEN

contract_cells.rule: per-source clocks at the pinned defaults: chat 900 s from the player's last chat claim; grid 600 s from the player's last grid fire; no cross-surface coupling
one shared per-player clock c=600 s: any encounter on either surface blocks BOTH surfaces for 600 s
one shared per-player clock c=900 s: any encounter on either surface blocks BOTH surfaces for 900 s
per-source clocks at the pinned defaults PLUS a combined per-player cap: at most K=4 encounters in any sliding 3600 s window
per-source clocks at the pinned defaults PLUS a combined per-player cap: at most K=6 encounters in any sliding 3600 s window
per-source clocks at the pinned defaults PLUS a combined per-player cap: at most K=8 encounters in any sliding 3600 s window

profiles.regression_legs_first: chat-only (V001 tiers at (24,30,300) visibility + lone farmer at (24,30,900)) and grid-only (V008 styles at (15,0.02,600), 1 h AND 8 h) must reproduce the parents' published headline numbers EXACTLY (self-check-enforced) before any joint cell counts — the VERDICT 017 baseline-leg discipline

joint.chat: V001 med-tier honest channel (3 msg/min, 8 users), joint player = author 0
V001 med-tier honest channel (3 msg/min, 8 users), joint player = author 0
V001 lone paced-spam farmer (gap = debounce = 30 s, own channel — V001's antifarm leg)
joint.grid: V008 !fastmine grinder trace (parked deep, ~3 s cadence), same seed
joint.note: runs both surfaces in parallel — each surface's cooldown dead time is filled with the other surface's actions; the cross-surface arbitrage channel neither solo sim could see

profiles.joint_solo_anchors_note: joint-leg per-surface comparisons use the SAME-horizon (8 h) solo-leg values computed from the same vendored trace models, so deltas are contract effects, not horizon effects

scoring.P1_wild_shape: med-tier channel spawns/hr stays in V001's rare-but-visible band [0.5, 6.0] (the band VERDICT 001's report explicitly defined) for both honest mixed profiles, under the 5.22/hr debounce x threshold ceiling
scoring.P2_grid_shape: zero fires below the depth threshold on every profile/seed (structural gating), mixed-deep grid enc/hr > 0 (rare-but-present at depth), and every profile's grid enc/hr <= 6.0 + eps (V008's solo cap)
scoring.P3_chat_farm_cap: every profile's chat claims/hr <= 4.0 + eps (V001's per-claimer solo cap — a contract whose namespace rule lets chat claims exceed the verdicted per-claimer cap re-pins V001)
scoring.P4_combined_bound: every profile's combined interruptions/hr (chat claims + grid fires, per player) <= 4.0 + eps — the stricter solo ceiling min(4, 6), the Q-0087 never-mandatory bound named by the proposal
scoring.P5_no_material_arbitrage: arb-farmer combined rate <= ARB_RATIO_MAX x max(honest mixed combined rate); ARB_RATIO_MAX = 2.0, committed from the only parent-measured farmer-vs-honest RATE precedent (VERDICT 008 accepted 1.86 bounded-and-shrinking at the pinned defaults; 2.0 is that precedent rounded up)
scoring.P6_no_rate_inflation: each honest mixed profile's per-surface rate <= its same-horizon solo-leg rate + 0.01/hr (coexistence must never RAISE a solo-tuned surface rate; drops are reported, not failed)

winner_rule: PASS(cell) iff P1..P6 all hold on every seed-mean. Among PASS cells prefer, in order: (1) preserves_pinned_defaults = true (both verdicts' solo default sets survive unchanged); (2) minimal honest-rate distortion = sum over honest mixed profiles and surfaces of |joint rate - same-horizon solo rate|; (3) the smallest added constraint (lowest K / longest shared c). If NO cell passes, the finding path: name the breached criterion per cell and the default or namespace rule that needs re-pinning. Committed BEFORE results existed.
reported_not_scored: combined encounter-mint share per profile (chat vs grid fraction, RATE terms only)
contract-blocked attempt counts per surface (own-clock vs cross-surface vs combined-cap)
arb-farmer lone-channel spawn dynamics (spawns left to expire under shared clocks)

walls_restated.reward_value: no live fishing/mining earn-rate baseline exists, so loot values stay provisional and the slice must log the same named telemetry — VERDICT 001's caveat, restated verbatim by VERDICT 008 and again here; every mint conclusion in this sim is RATE/fraction-only
walls_restated.explore_action: the third trigger (EXPLORE_ACTION, superbot-games quest beats) inherits the namespace rule by contract, not by this sweep — its pacing is lane-pinned

telemetry_extension_combined_fields: per player per day: combined_encounters_all_surfaces (count + per-trigger breakdown chat_activity/grid_roam)
per encounter: trigger namespace + player_id + fire_ts + cooldown_state_at_fire (per-source clocks remaining, combined-window count)
per player per day: cooldown_block_events per surface, split own-clock vs combined-cap
per player per day: combined encounter-mint share vs each surface's solo mint (RATE/fraction terms — the VALUE ratio stays unmeasurable until the parents' named earn-rate baseline telemetry lands)
